mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) signals expanding manufacturing activity and economic strength, which leads to higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is always active based on the direction of INDPRO changes.
failure_condition: If the correlation between INDPRO 1-month change and SPX 20-day forward return is not significantly positive (Pearson r < 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window, or if INDPRO declines while SPX still rises.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above 0.5% after a period of near-zero rates, it signals the end of the accommodative cycle and monetary tightening begins. Higher discount rates compress equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 produces positive returns in 6 out of 10 instances when FEDFUNDS rises above 0.5% from below, or if the average return after trigger is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: The joint condition of high unemployment (UNRATE above 8%) and low inflation (CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth below 2%) indicates persistent economic slack without inflationary pressures, allowing the Federal Reserve to maintain accommodative monetary policy. This combination supports equity prices and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If the combination of UNRATE > 8% and CPIAUCSL yoy < 2% does not produce a median forward return significantly greater than the unconditional median over any 2-year window, or if the joint signal's predictive power is reducible to either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-crisis recovery regime, housing starts (HOUST) serve as a leading indicator of economic revival. Rising housing construction boosts related industries and consumer confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. This signal is most potent during early-cycle recovery phases.
failure_condition: If the correlation between HOUST changes and SPX 20-day returns is not positive during periods identified as post-crisis recovery (e.g., 2009 onward), or if the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold strategy in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) are a direct measure of consumer spending, a primary driver of GDP. Increases in RSAFS indicate improving household consumption and economic momentum, which translate into higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is always active and does not require a threshold condition.
failure_condition: If the rank correlation between RSAFS monthly change and SPX 20-day forward return is below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling period, or if 60% of RSAFS declines are followed by positive SPX returns.

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly real GDP growth (annualized rate computed from GDPC1) exceeds 2%, it signals that the economy is expanding at a healthy pace, driving corporate profits and investor optimism. This threshold-triggered condition predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If instances where GDP growth exceeds 2% do not yield a median forward 60-day return above the unconditional median, or if the hit rate (fraction of positive returns after trigger) is below 55% in a walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Joint increases in nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) and industrial production (INDPRO) confirm broad-based economic recovery. Rising employment supports income and consumption while rising production signals business investment. This dual confirmation is a powerful predictor of positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days, and the signal is not reducible to either series individually.
failure_condition: If the joint signal (both PAYEMS and INDPRO increasing month-over-month) does not produce a median forward return significantly greater than the single-series signals, or if the predictive power is fully captured by either series alone in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero-interest-rate regime (FEDFUNDS near 0%), any positive surprise in CPI (CPIAUCSL) is interpreted as a sign of healing demand and reflation, rather than a tightening risk, because the central bank is expected to keep rates low. This short-horizon signal yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If in periods with FEDFUNDS <= 0.25%, a monthly increase in CPIAUCSL does not lead to a positive median 5-day forward return, or if the signal is negative in a majority of instances.
